title: Fix MySQL Lock Wait Timeout on Object Field Updates
description:
## Bug
A MySQL lock wait timeout error occurs when updating records in the `dtp_object_fields` table:

```
SQLSTATE: General error: 1205 Lock wait timeout exceeded; try restarting transaction
```

The error occurs in `ScorecardsController.php` on line 278 during an UPDATE operation on `dtp_object_fields` setting metadata fields like `ExtraTypeInfo`, `IsCalculated`, `IsPolymorphicForeignKey`, `LastModifiedById`, `LastModifiedDate`, and `field_definition_data_type`.

## Root Cause
Concurrent transactions are holding locks on the `dtp_object_fields` table, causing subsequent updates to time out waiting for the lock.

## Investigation
Need to identify what other processes are locking these rows and implement proper transaction management or retry logic.
acceptance_criteria: Object field updates complete without lock wait timeout errors
Concurrent update scenarios are handled gracefully
No data loss or corruption from retry logic
middleware_changes: Fix transaction management or add retry logic in ScorecardsController.php for dtp_object_fields updates to handle lock contention.
implementation_notes: MySQL InnoDB lock wait timeout (default 50s) is being exceeded. The UPDATE on dtp_object_fields at line 278 of ScorecardsController.php is blocked by concurrent transactions. Need to investigate lock contention and add transaction retry logic or optimize locking strategy.
